PC Component Library

Mission Brief

How to Read Specs

Decode the numbers that matter when comparing hardware.

⚡

Clock Speed (GHz)

Measures how many cycles per second a processor completes. Higher GHz = faster single-core tasks. A 4.5 GHz CPU runs 4.5 billion cycles per second.

Performance Impact
🧵

Cores & Threads

Cores are independent processing units. Threads are virtual cores via Hyper-Threading. More cores = better multitasking and rendering performance.

Performance Impact
💾

VRAM (GB)

GPU memory used for textures, framebuffers, and models. 8GB handles 1080p/1440p gaming; 12–16GB is recommended for 4K and AI workloads.

Performance Impact
🔁

RAM Speed (MHz)

Data transfer rate of system memory. DDR5-6000 is faster than DDR4-3200. Affects latency and bandwidth, crucial for CPU-bound tasks.

Performance Impact
📡

TDP (Watts)

Thermal Design Power — how much heat a chip generates under load. Higher TDP needs better cooling. Affects power consumption and case airflow.

Cooling Requirement
🚀

PCIe Generation

Data pathway between CPU, GPU, and NVMe drives. PCIe 5.0 doubles bandwidth vs PCIe 4.0 — critical for top-end NVMe SSDs and future GPUs.

Bandwidth Impact
Side by Side

CPU vs GPU — Core Differences

Understanding when each processor excels.

Attribute CPU GPU
Core Count4–64 coresThousands of shader units
Primary JobSequential, complex logicParallel, repetitive tasks
Clock Speed3–6 GHz per core1–3 GHz (shader clock)
CacheL1/L2/L3 (up to 256MB)L1/L2 on-chip, VRAM off-chip
Best ForGaming, OS, productivityRendering, AI, video encoding
MemorySystem RAM (DDR5)Dedicated VRAM (GDDR7)
Power Range65W – 250W75W – 600W
